PICO IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, ISSUE AND EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS, AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS (TRANSACTION ID # 59299153) FILED BY DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER (ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS' MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, ISSUE AND EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS, AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS. ARGUED AND SUBMITTED BY BOTH SIDES. THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. WHILE PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL'S DISCOVERY CONDUCT APPEARS TO HAVE BEEN LAMENTABLE, TERMINATION OF PLAINTIFF'S ENTIRE CASE WOULD BE TOO DRACONIAN FOR THESE CIRCUMSTANCES. I WOULD HAVE BEEN INCLINED TO GRANT ISSUE AND/OR EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS. HOWEVER, DEFENDANT'S MOTION FAILS TO ARTICULATE WHAT THOSE SPECIFIC SANCTIONS SHOULD BE OR LINK THOSE PROPOSED SANCTIONS TO PARTICULAR UNANSWERED DISCOVERY. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES HUNTER, CSR #6576

MOTION FOR RELIEF, CCP SEC. 664.6 (TRANSACTION ID # 100065858) FILED BY PLAINTIFF ALMIROL, BRETT HEARING SET FOR APR-09-2019 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF BRETT ALMIROL'S MOTION FOR RELIEF, CCP SEC. 664.6. PLAINTIFF BRETT ALMIROL'S "MOTION FOR RELIEF, CCP SEC. 664.6" IS DENIED. IN VIOLATION OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE AND THE RULES OF COURT, PLAINTIFF'S MOVING PAPERS DO NOT CONTAIN A MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, A SUPPORTING DECLARATION, A COPY OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT PLAINTIFF CLAIMS WAS ENTERED INTO ON JANUARY 31, 2018, OR EVEN A SUMMARY OF ITS TERMS. THEY ARE WOEFULLY INADEQUATE TO MEET PLAINTIFF'S BURDEN TO SHOW ENTITLEMENT TO ANY RELIEF. IN ADDITION, DEFENDANT TOTAL RENAL CARE, INC.'S SHOWING ESTABLISHES THAT PLAINTIFF IS IMPROPERLY REQUESTING THAT THE COURT ALTER THE TERMS OF THE PARTIES' SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT, RATHER THAN ENFORCE IT PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 664.6, AND THAT PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO COMPLY WITH HIS OBLIGATIONS UNDER THAT AGREEMENT. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-02-2019 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed, the Court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. JUDGE GARRETT L. WONG; Clerk JLee. Proceedings not reported. (610)

Motion For Relief, Ccp Sec. 664.6
Off Calendar Apr-09-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF BRETT ALMIROL'S Motion For Relief, Ccp Sec. 664.6.

Plaintiff Brett Almirol's "Motion for Relief, CCP Sec. 664.6" is denied. In violation of the Code of Civil Procedure and the Rules of Court, Plaintiff's moving papers do not contain a memorandum of points and authorities, a supporting declaration, a copy of the settlement agreement Plaintiff claims was entered into on January 31, 2018, or even a summary of its terms. They are woefully inadequate to meet Plaintiff's burden to show entitlement to any relief. In addition, Defendant Total Renal Care, Inc.'s showing establishes that Plaintiff is improperly requesting that the Court alter the terms of the parties' settlement agreement, rather than enforce it pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6, and that Plaintiff has failed to comply with his obligations under that agreement.

Counsel for Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Or, In The Alternative, To Correct Mistake In Entry Of Dismissal Of California Pacific Medical Center Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 473
Off Calendar Oct-26-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kristen Pico, Esq. (415-288-9800) of Hassard Bonnington LLP for Defendant SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS' Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Or, In The Alternative, To Correct Mistake In Entry Of Dismissal Of California Pacific Medical Center Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 473.

Defendant Sutter West Bay Hospitals' motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted. The dismissal of defendant California Pacific Medical Center had the effect, if not the intent, of also dismissing Sutter West Bay Hospitals. Sutter West Bay Hospitals is dismissed from this case. Plaintiff Brett Amirol knew for many months prior to his filing of a dismissal of CPMC that it was the fictitious name of Sutter West Bay Hospitals and that the two names refer to the same legal entity. Mr. Amirol fails to explain why the dismissal of CPMC does not also apply to Sutter West Bay Hospitals. Moreover, if Mr. Amirol intended to proceed against Sutter West Bay Hospitals, he fails to explain why he dismissed its fictitious name CPMC and did not seek to amend to add Sutter West Bay Hospitals as a defendant.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D302)

Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions, Or In The Alternative, Issue And Evidentiary Sanctions, And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-08-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, John E. Stringel, Esq. ((415-934-1827) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Kristen A. Pico of Hassard Bonnington LLP (415-288-9800) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS' Motion For Terminating Sanctions, Or In The Alternative, Issue And Evidentiary Sanctions, And For Monetary Sanctions is is DENIED. While plaintiff's counsel's discovery conduct appears to have been lamentable, termination of plaintiff's entire case would be too draconian for these circumstances. I would have been inclined to grant issue and/or evidentiary sanctions. However, defendant's motion fails to articulate what those specific sanctions should be or link those proposed sanctions to particular unanswered discovery. Prevailing party to submit a [proposed] written order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, Bus. Tel: 530-339-5023, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)

Notice Of Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-10-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. KRISTEN A. PICO, ESQ. of Hassard Bonnington LLP, Tel No. 415-288-9800 appearing for Defendant Sutter West Bay Hospitals;
No other appearances made in this matter.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS' Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint, Defendant Sutter West Bay Hospitals' motion to strike the punitive damages allegations is granted. Union Western Medical Centers v. Superior Court (1996) 42 Cal. App. 4th 500 establishes that the punitive damages allegations made by Mr. Alimor require pre-approval by the Court per Code of Civil Procedure 425.13. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
